subject: English
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the importance of polite and effective communication
Identify appropriate phrases for different social situations
Learn the value of empathy and thinking before speaking
Recognize how words can impact others' feelings
Develop basic conversational skills and etiquette
Practice using 'magic words' and polite expressions in context

lesson_plan.title: The Power of Words
topics_covered: How words make us feel (Happy vs. Sad words)
teaching_strategy: Class discussion: 'Tell us about a time someone said something nice to you'
Use a 'Word Cloud' on the board to categorize positive and negative speech
Introduction to the chapter's main theme
student_activities: Draw a 'Speech Bubble' and write a kind sentence to a classmate
Identify 'Happy Words' in a given list

lesson_plan.title: Magic Words and Manners
topics_covered: Using Please, Thank You, Sorry, and Excuse Me
teaching_strategy: Guided reading of the first section of the chapter
Teacher demonstration of polite vs. impolite requests
student_activities: Role-play a simple shopkeeper-customer interaction using magic words
Complete a matching worksheet: Situation vs. Appropriate Phrase

lesson_plan.title: Listening: The Other Half of Speaking
topics_covered: Importance of being a good listener
teaching_strategy: Game: 'Telephone' (to show how messages change when we don't listen)
Discussion on why interrupting is 'Saying the Wrong Thing'
student_activities: Write 3 rules for being a good listener in their notebooks

lesson_plan.title: Situational Success
topics_covered: Saying the right thing during a mistake
teaching_strategy: Scenario-based learning: 'What would you say if...?'
Teacher explanation of a 'Three-Part Apology' (I'm sorry, It was my fault, How can I fix it?)
Highlighting the importance of tone of voice
student_activities: Pair-work: Practice apologizing for a broken pencil

lesson_plan.title: Thinking Before Speaking
topics_covered: The 'THINK' filter (True, Helpful, Inspiring, Necessary, Kind)
teaching_strategy: Introduction to the 'THINK' acronym using a colorful poster
Discussion: 'Is it okay to say something if it's true but mean?'
Reading short stories from the chapter about social mistakes
student_activities: Color and fill in their own 'THINK' bookmark
Classify sentences as 'Think before saying' or 'Say it right away'

lesson_plan.title: Review and Communication Quiz
teaching_strategy: Vocabulary Bingo with phrases from the chapter
Reviewing the 'Magic Words' one last time
student_activities: Take a short quiz on matching situations with 'The Right Thing' to say

teaching_aids: Smile/Frown emoji paddles for 'Right or Wrong' game
teacher_tip: Model the behavior yourself throughout the week! Use 'The Right Thing' words with your students constantly. When a student uses a polite phrase spontaneously, give them a 'Communication Star' to reinforce the habit.
